message.content:
You are mapping the LESSON GENERATION subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/NodeLessonContent.model.ts
- server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts
- server/src/services/roadmapLesson.service.ts
Goal: explain how a lesson for a roadmap node is generated and whether it is reused. Specifically:
- What triggers lesson generation? Is a lesson generated per-node-globally (shared across all students) or per-user?
- What is the cache key for NodeLessonContent (subject? node? book? version?)? Is there versioning/invalidation?
- What does the AI prompt for a lesson look like — what source material is fed in (paragraph text? topic title? KTP?)?
- Is the lesson regenerated from scratch each time or fetched if it exists?
- How does lesson content relate to test generation downstream?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. Quote the cache-lookup logic and the model schema in evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I have everything needed. Let me compile the structured map.

input.subsystem: Lesson Generation (roadmap node lessons) — AI-consolidated lesson content for КТП roadmap nodes, cached in the node_lesson_content collection, served via GET /roadmaps/nodes/:nodeId/lesson

entities.name: NodeLessonContent (Mongoose model / INodeLessonContent)
fields: ktpTopicId: ObjectId (required; the КТП topic id, NOT a book/chapter id)
sourceHash: String (sha256 of mapped source topic texts — the invalidation key)
relationships: ktpTopicId -> logical ref into KtpCatalog topics (plain ObjectId, no populate)
NO userId field — document is per-(subject,ktpTopic), shared across ALL students
entities.notes: Compound UNIQUE index { subjectId:1, ktpTopicId:1 } (line 36) => exactly one cached lesson set per subject+КТП-topic globally. collection: 'node_lesson_content'. This is the cache table; the roadmap graph itself is built live from КТП and is NOT stored here.

entities.name: StoredLessonSchema (embedded canonical lesson / ICanonicalNodeLesson)
relationships: runtime ICanonicalNodeLesson also carries video?: IRoadmapLessonVideo|null and source?: ICanonicalNodeSource, but those are NOT persisted in StoredLessonSchema (video is re-set to null on read at nodeLessonContent.service.ts:63)
entities.notes: Stored shape is intentionally a subset of the runtime lesson type — no video, no source persisted.

entities.name: ICanonicalNodeSource (node source descriptor)
relationships: lives in node.metadata.sources[] on the live КТП-built canonical node
resolved to actual textbook text via extractTopicText(subject, bookId, chapterId, topicId)
entities.notes: These are the raw textbook-paragraph references that feed the AI consolidation prompt; multiple sources (different grades/books) per one КТП topic.

flows.name: Lesson resolve + generate + cache (resolveNodeLessons)
steps: getLesson loads Subject, resolves the live canonical node (roadmapLesson.service.ts:113 via roadmapService.resolveCanonical), then calls resolveNodeLessons(subjectId, subject, node) (roadmapLesson.service.ts:114)

resolveNodeLessons reads node.metadata.ktpTopicId and node.metadata.sources (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:98-99); if no ktpTopicId or no sources -> returns raw getNodeLessons(node) fallback, NOT cached (line 100)
Builds source texts: sourcesWithText() pulls textbook paragraph text via extractTopicText(subject, bookId, chapterId, topicId) (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:35-48); empty -> raw fallback (line 103)
Computes sourceHash = sha256(JSON of [[topicId,text],...]) (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:50-52, called line 105)

CACHE LOOKUP: NodeLessonContent.findOne({ subjectId, ktpTopicId }); HIT if row has lessons AND (generatedBy==='manual' OR sourceHash===hash) -> return storedToLessons(row) (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-110)
MISS / stale hash: if no process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY -> raw getNodeLessons fallback, not cached (line 112-114)
GENERATE: roadmapAIService.consolidateLessonContent({ subjectTitle, nodeTitle, sources:[{label,text}] }) (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:118-122); on throw -> raw fallback, not cached (line 123-126)
Map AI lessons to ICanonicalNodeLesson with lessonId=`${node.nodeId}::c${i}` (line 128-136)

CACHE WRITE: NodeLessonContent.findOneAndUpdate({subjectId,ktpTopicId}, {$set:{...,sourceHash:hash,generatedBy:'ai',lessons}}, {upsert:true}) (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:138-157); write failure is swallowed (.catch)
getLesson then picks target lesson, normalizes content, and lazily generates a per-lesson summary if missing (roadmapLesson.service.ts:132-146)
flows.aiCalls: consolidateLessonContent at roadmap.ai.service.ts:370 — OpenAI gpt-4o-mini (MODEL const, roadmap.ai.service.ts:7), temperature 0.3, max_tokens 3000, POST https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions. Prompt (roadmap.ai.service.ts:390-404) feeds: subjectTitle, КТП node/topic title, and the raw TEXTBOOK PARAGRAPH TEXT of each mapped source (### Источник: <bookTitle · topicTitle>\n<text>, total truncated to MAX_TOTAL=24000 chars). Asks for strict JSON { lessons:[{title,summary,content(Markdown)}] }, dedup across grade-level books, no invented facts. NOT cached at the AI layer — caching is the NodeLessonContent row keyed by sourceHash.

flows.name: Lesson summary generation (lazy, on read)
steps: In getLesson, if target.summary empty AND content non-empty AND OPENAI_API_KEY set -> roadmapAIService.generateLessonSummary (roadmapLesson.service.ts:134-140)

If subject is NOT live-built-from-КТП, persistLessonSummary writes summary back into the stored CanonicalRoadmap node metadata.lessons[] (roadmapLesson.service.ts:41-79); for live-КТП subjects it returns early (line 47) so the summary is recomputed each request
flows.aiCalls: generateLessonSummary at roadmap.ai.service.ts:233 — gpt-4o-mini, temp 0.35, max_tokens 600, prompt feeds subjectTitle+nodeTitle+lessonText (truncated 12000 chars), returns Markdown <=900 chars. For live-КТП subjects this summary is NOT persisted, so it re-runs per request.

flows.name: Lesson chat (per node, not generation but uses lesson text)
steps: POST /roadmaps/nodes/:nodeId/lesson/... -> roadmapLessonService.postChatMessage (roadmapLesson.service.ts:197) re-resolves lessons via resolveNodeLessons and passes lessonText to AI
flows.aiCalls: chatLessonNode at roadmap.ai.service.ts:292 — gpt-4o-mini, temp 0.45, supports image_url parts; constrained to the lesson text as the only theory source.

flows.name: Downstream node TEST generation (does NOT use lesson content)
steps: Content is sourced by resolveBookContentForAI(dto) (subjectContent.service.ts:10) which reads BOOK/CHAPTER text directly (subject.getBookContent / getChapterContent, lines 37/43) using dto.bookId/chapterId/topicFocus — it does NOT read NodeLessonContent or the consolidated lesson
Roadmap node only gates access (roadmapService.assertKnowledgeMapTestAllowed, test.controller.ts:266) and records results (recordTestSubmitted)
flows.aiCalls: aiService.generateTest at ai.service.ts:60 — gpt-4o-mini. Crucially the test prompt input is raw book/chapter text + topicFocus, independent of the AI-consolidated lesson. Lesson content and test content are generated from the same textbook source but via SEPARATE pipelines and SEPARATE caches (Test docs keyed by sourceContentHash vs NodeLessonContent keyed by sourceHash).

input.reuse: Lesson content IS reused/shared globally, not per-user. The NodeLessonContent document is keyed by (subjectId, ktpTopicId) with a unique compound index (NodeLessonContent.model.ts:36) and has no userId — so the AI-consolidated lesson is generated once and served to every student of that subject. Cache key for content version = sourceHash, a sha256 over the mapped source topics' [topicId,text] pairs (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:50-52). Invalidation is by content change only: on read, a stored row is reused if generatedBy==='manual' (never invalidated) OR sourceHash===freshly-computed-hash; otherwise it regenerates and upserts the same row (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-110, 138-157). There is no numeric version field on NodeLessonContent and no explicit cache-bust endpoint — editing/remapping the source textbook topics changes the hash and triggers regeneration on the next request. Per-lesson summaries are cached inside the lessons (or persisted to CanonicalRoadmap for non-live maps), but for live-КТП subjects the summary is recomputed every read (roadmapLesson.service.ts:47, 134-146). Test generation does NOT reuse lesson content at all — it has its own Test cache keyed by sourceContentHash/dailyPackId.
input.aiCost: AI cost is incurred per (subject, КТП-topic) lesson the FIRST time any student opens it (or after the source hash changes), NOT per user and NOT per request — the consolidateLessonContent OpenAI call (gpt-4o-mini) runs only on cache miss/stale and the result is shared across all students (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-159). Additional recurring cost: the lazy generateLessonSummary call (gpt-4o-mini) runs on read whenever the target lesson has no summary; for live-КТП subjects the summary is never persisted (persistLessonSummary early-returns, roadmapLesson.service.ts:47), so this summary call is paid on essentially every lesson view per user. Lesson chat (chatLessonNode) is per-message per-user. Downstream test generation cost is separate and content comes from raw book text, not the lesson.
problems: Lesson summary is regenerated on EVERY read for live-КТП subjects: isLiveBuiltFromKtp() makes persistLessonSummary early-return (roadmapLesson.service.ts:47), and consolidateLessonContent's summary is only stored in NodeLessonContent.lessons[].summary if the AI returned one — when it didn't, generateLessonSummary fires per request per user with no cache (roadmapLesson.service.ts:134-146). Real recurring cost + latency on the hot lesson-open path.

Lesson content and node test content are two independent pipelines reading the same textbook: lessons via extractTopicText on node.metadata.sources (nodeLessonContent.service.ts:44), tests via resolveBookContentForAI/getBookContent (subjectContent.service.ts:37,43). They can drift — a student studies the AI-consolidated/deduped lesson but is tested on raw book/chapter text selected by bookId/topicFocus string matching (subjectContent.service.ts:26-34), so questions may cover material not in the lesson and vice versa.

generatedBy:'manual' lessons are never invalidated regardless of source changes (sourceHash is ignored for manual rows, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:108) — stale manual content can silently persist after the textbook is edited.

No versioning/audit on NodeLessonContent: regeneration overwrites the single row in place (findOneAndUpdate upsert, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:138); no history, no version number, and the AI model/prompt version is not part of the cache key, so a prompt/model upgrade does NOT invalidate existing cached lessons.

sourceHash hashes full source texts (computeSourceHash, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:50-52) but consolidateLessonContent truncates inputs to MAX_TOTAL=24000 chars (roadmap.ai.service.ts:378-387). Tiny edits in a truncated-away tail still change the hash and force a costly regen that produces identical output.

Cache write failures are swallowed (.catch on the findOneAndUpdate, nodeLessonContent.service.ts:157): a persistent write error (e.g. duplicate-key race on the unique index from two concurrent first-readers) means every request re-pays the OpenAI consolidation with no error surfaced.
Concurrency: two students opening a never-generated node simultaneously both miss the cache and both call consolidateLessonContent (no lock/dedup), doubling AI cost on cold start.

Brittle AI-output parsing: parseJsonObject does indexOf('{')/lastIndexOf('}') slicing (roadmap.ai.service.ts:9-16); malformed/multi-object responses throw and fall back to raw, uncached sources, degrading the lesson silently.

stripHtmlToText / normalizeLessonContent (roadmapLesson.service.ts:20-32) collapses all whitespace with /\s+/ -> ' ', which would destroy Markdown structure (code blocks, lists) — currently only applied to html-format content, but a fragile coupling if stored format ever changes.
evidence: NodeLessonContent.model.ts:25-36 — const NodeLessonContentSchema = new Schema<Doc>({ subjectId: {type: ObjectId, ref:'Subject', required:true, index:true}, ktpTopicId: {type: ObjectId, required:true}, sourceHash: {type: String, required:true}, lessons: {type:[StoredLessonSchema], default:[]}, generatedBy: {type:String, enum:['ai','manual'], default:'ai'} }, {timestamps:true, collection:'node_lesson_content'}); NodeLessonContentSchema.index({ subjectId:1, ktpTopicId:1 }, { unique:true });

nodeLessonContent.service.ts:107-110 — const row = await NodeLessonContent.findOne({ subjectId, ktpTopicId }); if (row && row.lessons?.length && (row.generatedBy === 'manual' || row.sourceHash === hash)) { return storedToLessons(row); }

nodeLessonContent.service.ts:138-157 — await NodeLessonContent.findOneAndUpdate({ subjectId, ktpTopicId }, { $set: { subjectId, ktpTopicId, sourceHash: hash, generatedBy: 'ai', lessons: lessons.map(...) } }, { upsert: true, new: true }).catch((e)=>console.warn('cache write failed', e));
nodeLessonContent.service.ts:44 — const text = extractTopicText(subject, s.bookId, s.chapterId, s.topicId) || s.title || '';  (lesson source = textbook paragraph text)

roadmap.ai.service.ts:390-404 — prompt = ['Ниже — фрагменты учебников разных классов по ОДНОЙ теме. Собери из них единый урок...', ..., `Предмет: ${input.subjectTitle}. Тема: ${input.nodeTitle}.`, 'Источники:', sourcesBlock] where sourcesBlock blocks are `### Источник: ${s.label}\n${t}` (roadmap.ai.service.ts:386)
roadmap.ai.service.ts:7 — const MODEL = 'gpt-4o-mini';  and consolidate call roadmap.ai.service.ts:409-411 — model: MODEL, temperature: 0.3, max_tokens: 3000

test.controller.ts:274,283 — const { contentForAI, book } = await resolveBookContentForAI(dto); ... const generated = await aiService.generateTest(contentForAI, previousQuestions, ...) — node test content comes from book, not NodeLessonContent
subjectContent.service.ts:37,43 — contentText = subject.getBookContent(dto.bookId) / subject.getChapterContent(dto.bookId, resolvedChapterId!) — test source is raw book/chapter text

roadmapLesson.service.ts:134-146 — let summary = target.summary?.trim() ?? ''; if (!summary && contentNormalized.trim() && process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY) { summary = await roadmapAIService.generateLessonSummary({...}); if (summary) await persistLessonSummary(...); }  combined with roadmapLesson.service.ts:47 — if (await isLiveBuiltFromKtp(subjectId)) return; (summary not persisted for live КТП)

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
I've mapped the lesson generation subsystem. Summary of key findings:

**Trigger & sharing model:** Lesson generation is triggered lazily on `GET /roadmaps/nodes/:nodeId/lesson` (auth route, `roadmap.routes.ts:56` → `roadmapLessonService.getLesson` → `resolveNodeLessons`). It is **per-node-globally / shared across all students**, not per-user — the `NodeLessonContent` document has no `userId` and a unique compound index `{ subjectId:1, ktpTopicId:1 }` (`NodeLessonContent.model.ts:36`).

**Cache key & versioning:** Keyed by `(subjectId, ktpTopicId)` — i.e. subject + КТП topic, **not** book/chapter and **not** a numeric version. Content versioning is via `sourceHash` = sha256 of the mapped source topics' `[topicId, text]` pairs (`nodeLessonContent.service.ts:50-52`). Invalidation is content-driven only: a row is reused if `generatedBy==='manual'` OR `sourceHash` matches (`:107-110`); otherwise it regenerates and upserts in place. No version history, and the AI model/prompt is not part of the key.

**AI prompt source material:** `consolidateLessonContent` (`roadmap.ai.service.ts:370`, gpt-4o-mini, temp 0.3, max_tokens 3000) is fed the subject title, the КТП node/topic title, and the **raw textbook paragraph text** of each mapped source (`extractTopicText(subject, bookId, chapterId, topicId)`), truncated to 24000 chars total. It is asked to merge/dedup across grade-level books into Markdown lessons. Not the КТП structure text itself — the actual book paragraph text.

**Regenerated vs fetched:** Fetched if a valid cached row exists; regenerated from scratch only on cache miss or stale hash. Without `OPENAI_API_KEY` or on AI error it returns a raw, uncached fallback (`getNodeLessons`).

**Downstream test relationship:** Tests do **not** use the consolidated lesson. Node test generation (`test.controller.ts:274/283`) sources content via `resolveBookContentForAI` → `subject.getBookContent/getChapterContent` (raw book/chapter text). The roadmap node only gates and records tests. So lessons and tests are two independent AI pipelines over the same textbook with separate caches — a notable drift risk.

Relevant files (all absolute):
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/NodeLessonContent.model.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/nodeLessonContent.service.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/roadmapLesson.service.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/utils/nodeLessons.util.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/types/roadmap.types.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts
